heading: Advanced microelectronics research and development
label: 4656 Advanced microelectronics research and development

children.heading: Establishment required
children.label: 1 Establishment required
children.text: The President shall establish in the National Science and Technology Council a subcommittee on matters relating to leadership and competitiveness of the United States in microelectronics technology and innovation (in this section referred to as the “Subcommittee)”.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary of Defense.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary of Energy.

children.label: C
children.text: The Director of the National Science Foundation.

children.label: D
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce.

children.label: E
children.text: The Secretary of State.

children.label: F
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security.

children.label: G
children.text: The United States Trade Representative.

children.label: H
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence.

children.label: I
children.text: The heads of such other departments and agencies of the Federal Government as the President determines appropriate.

children.heading: National strategy on microelectronics research
children.label: A National strategy on microelectronics research
I
children.text: accelerate the domestic development and production of microelectronics and strengthen the domestic microelectronics workforce; and

children.label: II
children.text: ensure that the United States is a global leader in the field of microelectronics research and development.

children.label: I
children.text: activities that may be carried out to strengthen engagement and outreach between the Department of Defense and industry, academia, international partners of the United States, and other departments and agencies of the Federal Government on issues relating to microelectronics;

children.label: II
children.text: priorities for research and development to accelerate the advancement and adoption of innovative microelectronics and new uses of microelectronics and components, including for technologies based on organic and inorganic materials;

children.label: III
children.text: the role of diplomacy and trade in maintaining the position of the United States as a global leader in the field of microelectronics;

children.label: IV
children.text: the potential role of a Federal laboratory, center, or incubator exclusively focused on the research and development of microelectronics, as described in section 231(b)(15) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2017 (as added by section 276 of this Act) in carrying out the strategy and plan required under this subparagraph; and

children.label: V
children.text: such other activities as the Subcommittee determines may be appropriate to overcome future challenges to the innovation, competitiveness, supply chain integrity, and workforce development of the United States in the field of microelectronics.

children.heading: Fostering coordination of research and development
children.label: B Fostering coordination of research and development
children.text: The Subcommittee shall coordinate microelectronics related research, development, manufacturing, and supply chain security activities and budgets of Federal agencies and ensure such activities are consistent with the strategy required under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Progress briefing
children.label: i Progress briefing
children.text: Not later than one year after , the President shall provide to the appropriate committees of Congress a briefing on the progress of the Subcommittee in developing the strategy required under subparagraph (A).January 1, 2021

children.heading: Strategy update
children.label: ii Strategy update
children.text: Not less frequently than once every 5 years, the Subcommittee shall update the strategy developed under subparagraph (A) and submit the revised strategy to the appropriate committees of Congress.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: 4 Sunset
children.text: The Subcommittee shall terminate on the date that is 10 years after .January 1, 2021

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary of Energy, and the Secretary of Homeland Security, shall establish an advisory committee to be composed of not fewer than 12 members, including representatives of industry, federal laboratories, and academic institutions, who are qualified to provide advice to the United States Government on matters relating to microelectronics research, development, manufacturing, and policy.

children.label: A
children.text: science and technology needs of the nation’s domestic microelectronics industry;

children.label: B
children.text: the extent to which the strategy developed under subsection (a)(3) is helping maintain United States leadership in microelectronics manufacturing;

children.label: C
children.text: assessment of the research and development programs and activities authorized under this section; and

children.label: D
children.text: opportunities for new public-private partnerships to advance microelectronics research, development, and domestic manufacturing.

children.heading: FACA exemption
children.label: 3 FACA exemption
children.text: Section 14 of the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) shall not apply to the advisory committee established under this subsection.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations for such purpose, the Secretary of Commerce, in collaboration with the Secretary of Defense, shall establish a national semiconductor technology center to conduct research and prototyping of advanced semiconductor technology and grow the domestic semiconductor workforce to strengthen the economic competitiveness and security of the domestic supply chain. Such center shall be operated as a public private-sector consortium with participation from the private sector, the Department of Energy, and the National Science Foundation. The Secretary may make financial assistance awards, including construction awards, in support of the national semiconductor technology center.

children.label: i
children.text: Semiconductor advanced test, assembly, and packaging capability in the domestic ecosystem.

children.label: ii
children.text: Materials characterization, instrumentation and testing for next generation microelectronics.

children.label: iii
children.text: Virtualization and automation of maintenance of semiconductor machinery.

children.label: iv
children.text: Metrology for security and supply chain verification.

children.label: i
children.text: advanced metrology and characterization for manufacturing of microchips using 3 nanometer transistor processes or more advanced processes; and

children.label: ii
children.text: metrology for security and supply chain verification.

children.label: i
children.text: the development and dissemination of curricula and research training experiences; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the development of workforce training programs and apprenticeships in advanced microelectronic design, research, fabrication, and packaging capabilities.

children.heading: National Advanced Packaging Manufacturing Program
children.label: d National Advanced Packaging Manufacturing Program
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations for such purpose, the Secretary of Commerce shall establish a National Advanced Packaging Manufacturing Program led by the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology, in coordination with the national semiconductor technology center established under subsection (c), to strengthen semiconductor advanced test, assembly, and packaging capability in the domestic ecosystem, and which shall coordinate with a Manufacturing USA institute established under subsection (f), if applicable. The Director may make financial assistance awards, including construction awards, in support of the National Advanced Packaging Manufacturing Program.

children.heading: Microelectronics research at the National Institute of Standards and Technology
children.label: e Microelectronics research at the National Institute of Standards and Technology
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations for such purpose, the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology shall carry out a microelectronics research program to enable advances and breakthroughs in measurement science, standards, material characterization, instrumentation, testing, and manufacturing capabilities that will accelerate the underlying research and development for metrology of next generation microelectronics and ensure the competitiveness and leadership of the United States within this sector.

children.heading: Creation of a Manufacturing USA institute
children.label: f Creation of a Manufacturing USA institute

children.text: Research to support the virtualization and automation of maintenance of semiconductor machinery.
Development of new advanced test, assembly and packaging capabilities.
Developing and deploying educational and skills training curricula needed to support the industry sector and ensure the United States can build and maintain a trusted and predictable talent pipeline.

children.heading: Domestic production requirements
children.label: g Domestic production requirements
children.text: The head of any executive agency receiving funding under this section shall develop policies to require domestic production, to the extent possible, for any intellectual property resulting from microelectronics research and development conducted as a result of such funding and domestic control requirements to protect any such intellectual property from foreign adversaries.

children.heading: Construction projects
children.label: h Construction projects
children.text: shall apply to a construction project that receives financial assistance under this section.Section 3212 of title 42
